task_completion.description: Create the intersection geometry for Main St and Elm Ave
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a visual representation of a cross street or intersection drawn in the draw.io canvas.

task_completion.description: Include text labels for streets and a North arrow indicator
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text labels 'Main' and 'Elm' identifying the streets, and a directional arrow or compass symbol indicating 'North' or 'N'.

task_completion.description: Place Unit 1 (Blue Sedan) and Unit 2 (Red Truck) with text labels
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a blue vehicle shape or rectangle labeled 'Unit 1' and a red vehicle shape or rectangle labeled 'Unit 2' on the diagram.

task_completion.description: Accurately reflect the collision scenario at the point of impact
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the positioning of the vehicles: the blue vehicle (Unit 1) should be facing North, and its front bumper should be colliding with the right/passenger side of the red vehicle (Unit 2) at an approximate 45-degree angle, indicating Unit 2 was turning left.

task_completion.description: Save the diagram as 'accident_reconstruction.drawio' to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io application window title showing 'accident_reconstruction.drawio' or the .drawio file icon appearing on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Export the diagram as 'accident_reconstruction.png' to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the file 'accident_reconstruction.png' on the Desktop, an export dialog in draw.io, or an image viewer showing the exported PNG.

integrity.description: Agent used the draw.io Desktop application to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the draw.io GUI is open and actively used to place shapes and text. The agent should not be writing XML directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal windows to ensure the agent did not use Python scripts, ImageMagick, or other CLI tools to automatically generate the .drawio or .png files.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely exported the PNG from draw.io
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the draw.io 'File -> Export as -> PNG' menu or dialogs, rather than just renaming a file extension in the file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Unit 1 is a blue vehicle traveling northbound. Unit 2 is a red vehicle turning left. The impact is at approximately 45 degrees with the front of Unit 1 striking the passenger side of Unit 2. Required labels are 'Main', 'Elm', 'Unit 1', and 'Unit 2'. The output files must be named 'accident_reconstruction.drawio' and 'accident_reconstruction.png' and saved to the Desktop.
